on their land and they can
change the rent whenever they want That's the deal with every no-code platform You get speed but give up control
And that trade-off feels fine until it doesn't So I was sitting there looking at this new pricing I've got
months of work in this thing Leaving feels impossible but staying means paying 3 times more for a tool that
still can't build me a Kanban board So I did something I should have done a year ago I pulled

things that would have taken me weeks of workarounds in FlutterFlow Claude Code knocked them out one by one Some

described what I wanted and it built it And that's when I realized the problem was never me It wasn't—
it was never that I wasn't technical enough or creative enough Problem was the ceiling The two had a ceiling
and I kept banging my head on it And if FlutterFlow had the ceiling what else does Because when I

$3,000 just to start using using the thing and you're locked into an annual contract Realize after 2 months you

the real number is worse than you think Over $1,000
a month and I use maybe 20% of what I'm paying for So I actually sat down and I calculated

a month or £80
I use it every single day never hit the limit runs on the Opus model $80 versus over $1,000 for

build my own The email automation built it The forms with custom logic build those too Not janky prototypes production
tools that I actually use every day The difference is I own them now Nobody can triple my pricing Nobody

nobody can take it away SaaS made sense when building custom tools was expensive You needed developers you needed time
you needed money So you rented someone else's tool instead Building isn't expensive anymore The entire cost equation
has flipped But here's what nobody's talking about The AI coding tools the ones that are supposed to be the

Claude or GPT is at the bottom And in the middle there's a wrapper called Cursor Bolt whatever That wrapper

access
These companies aren't selling you AI they're selling you access to someone else's AI You're paying for the middleman not

coding tool And it's better because they made the AI they didn't need to figure out
from the outside or work around its limitations They just use it And you can already see the cracks And
the CEO admitted it publicly in June 2025 Cursor cut their Pro plan requests from 500 down to about 225
Their CEO had to publicly apologize And the reason was simple The underlying AI models from Anthropic and OpenAI got
more expensive So Cursor had to pass the cost on to you And it's not just Cursor Lovable charges you
to generate apps using the exact same Claude model you can access directly Bolt does the same thing They're all

And the middlemen who don't adapt disappear That's where we are right now with AI coding tools The question isn't
